recipes.title: Tacos al Pastor
recipes.description: Marinated pork shaved off a vertical spit and served on small corn tortillas with pineapple, onion, and cilantro.
steps: Toast the dried chiles briefly in a dry skillet, then soak in hot water for 15 minutes until soft.
Blend the chiles with achiote, vinegar, pineapple juice, garlic, cumin, oregano, and 1 tsp salt to a smooth marinade.
Coat the pork slices in the marinade and refrigerate at least 4 hours, ideally overnight.
Cook the marinated pork on a very hot griddle or grill in batches until charred at the edges, about 3 minutes per side, then chop finely.
On the same surface, char the diced pineapple briefly until lightly caramelized.
Warm the tortillas on a dry skillet until pliable.
Build each taco with chopped pork, a spoon of pineapple, onion, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime.

recipes.title: Enchiladas Rojas
recipes.description: Corn tortillas filled with shredded chicken, rolled, and bathed in red chile sauce, baked with cheese and crema.
steps: Toast the chiles in a dry pan, then simmer with tomatoes, half the onion, and garlic in water for 10 minutes.
Drain and blend with cumin, oregano, and 1 tsp salt to a smooth red sauce; strain.
Pour the sauce into a wide skillet and warm over low heat.
Lightly fry each tortilla in oil for 5 seconds per side to soften, then dip into the warm sauce.
Fill each sauced tortilla with shredded chicken, roll up, and place seam-side down in a baking dish.
Pour any remaining sauce over the top and bake at 190°C for 10 minutes until heated through.
Top with queso fresco, crema, finely chopped onion, and cilantro before serving.

recipes.title: Chiles Rellenos
recipes.description: Roasted poblano peppers stuffed with cheese, dipped in fluffy egg batter, fried, and served in tomato broth.
steps: Roast the poblanos directly over a flame until blackened all over, then steam in a covered bowl for 10 minutes; peel carefully, leaving stems intact.
Cut a slit lengthwise in each chile, remove seeds, and stuff with strips of queso Oaxaca.
Blend tomatoes, onion, and garlic, then simmer in a saucepan with stock and salt for 10 minutes for the broth.
Beat egg whites to stiff peaks, then gently fold in the yolks and a pinch of salt.
Heat 5 cm of oil to 175°C, dust each stuffed chile with flour, dip in the egg batter, and fry until deep golden, turning once, about 3 minutes total.
Drain on paper towels.
Serve each chile in a shallow bowl with the warm tomato broth ladled around it.

recipes.title: Mole Poblano
recipes.description: Complex Pueblan sauce of chiles, chocolate, spices, and seeds, traditionally served over turkey or chicken.
steps: Simmer the chicken in salted water with half the onion until just cooked, then reserve the broth.
Toast each variety of chile in a dry pan briefly, then soak in hot water 15 minutes.
In oil, separately toast sesame seeds, almonds, raisins, cinnamon, and cloves until fragrant.
Fry the torn tortilla until crisp.
Char tomatoes, remaining onion, and garlic on a comal until blistered.
Blend chiles, vegetables, toasted seeds and nuts, raisins, spices, tortilla, and chocolate with reserved broth in batches until very smooth.
Strain the mole into a heavy pot, simmer 30–40 minutes until thick and glossy, season, and serve ladled over the chicken with sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Tamales
recipes.description: Steamed corn masa parcels wrapped in corn husks, traditionally filled with red chile pork or chicken.
steps: Soak corn husks in hot water for 1 hour until pliable.
Toast and soak the chiles, then blend with garlic, cumin, oregano, and salt to a thick sauce; mix with shredded pork.
Beat the lard until fluffy, then beat in masa harina, baking powder, salt, and warm stock until a soft, spreadable dough forms.
Spread 3 tbsp of masa down the center of a husk, top with 2 tbsp filling, and fold the husk over to enclose, then fold up the bottom.
Stand tamales upright in a steamer with the open ends up.
Steam over simmering water for 75–90 minutes, replenishing water as needed, until the masa easily separates from the husk.
Rest 10 minutes before unwrapping.

recipes.title: Pozole Rojo
recipes.description: Hominy stew in a deep red chile broth with pork, garnished with cabbage, radish, lime, and oregano.
steps: Simmer pork with half the onion, 2 garlic cloves, bay leaves, and salt in 2 l water for 90 minutes until tender.
Toast the chiles, then soak in hot water 15 minutes.
Blend the soaked chiles with remaining onion and garlic, cumin, oregano, and a ladle of pork broth until smooth.
Strain the chile sauce into the pork pot.
Add hominy and simmer everything together 30 minutes; adjust salt.
Ladle into bowls and serve with cabbage, radish slices, lime wedges, oregano, and tostadas at the table.
Diners build their own bowls with the toppings.

recipes.title: Guacamole
recipes.description: Bright dip of mashed avocado with lime, white onion, jalapeño, cilantro, and salt.
steps: In a molcajete or sturdy bowl, mash a small portion of the onion, jalapeño, cilantro, and salt to a paste.
Halve the avocados, scoop the flesh into the bowl, and mash to your preferred chunky texture.
Stir in lime juice, the rest of the onion and jalapeño, and the tomato if using.
Taste and adjust salt and lime.
Serve immediately, ideally with warm tortilla chips.

recipes.title: Salsa Roja
recipes.description: Smoky charred-tomato salsa with chiles, garlic, and onion, perfect for tacos, eggs, or chips.
steps: Heat a comal or heavy skillet over medium-high heat.
Char the tomatoes, chiles, onion, and unpeeled garlic, turning until blistered and soft, 8–10 minutes.
Peel the garlic.
Blend the charred vegetables with cilantro and salt to your preferred texture, pulse for chunky or run smooth.
Stir in lime juice and adjust salt.
Serve at room temperature or warm slightly in a pan.

recipes.title: Chilaquiles Verdes
recipes.description: Crispy tortilla chips simmered briefly in green tomatillo salsa, topped with crema, onion, and queso fresco.

ingredients.name: stale corn tortillas, cut into wedges

steps: Heat oil and fry the tortilla wedges in batches until crisp and golden; drain on paper towels and salt lightly.
Boil tomatillos, chiles, half the onion, and garlic in water for 8 minutes until soft.
Drain and blend with cilantro, stock, and salt to a smooth green salsa.
Pour the salsa into a wide skillet and simmer for 5 minutes, adjusting consistency.
Add the fried tortillas and toss for 1–2 minutes so they soak up the salsa but stay slightly crisp.
Plate immediately and top with crema, queso fresco, finely chopped onion, and cilantro.
Crown each plate with a fried egg.

recipes.title: Carnitas
recipes.description: Michoacán-style pork shoulder slow-cooked in lard until tender, then crisped at the edges; served on tortillas.

ingredients.name: pork shoulder, cut into 5 cm chunks

steps: Place pork, lard, onion, garlic, oregano, bay leaves, and salt in a heavy pot.
Squeeze in the orange juice and add the spent halves; pour in the milk.
Add water to barely cover, bring to a simmer, then cook gently uncovered for 2.5 hours.
When the liquid has nearly evaporated, increase heat slightly and let the pork fry in its own rendered fat, turning to crisp the edges, 15–20 minutes.
Remove pork with a slotted spoon and shred coarsely.
Warm the tortillas on a comal.
Serve carnitas piled on tortillas with chopped onion, cilantro, salsa, and lime.

recipes.title: Tortilla Soup
recipes.description: Tomato and chicken broth-based soup topped with crispy tortilla strips, avocado, lime, and queso fresco.

ingredients.name: corn tortillas, cut into thin strips

steps: Char the tomatoes, half the onion, and garlic on a comal until blistered.
Toast and soak the guajillo chile briefly.
Blend the charred vegetables with the chiles and 240 ml stock until very smooth.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pot, pour in the puree, and fry for 5 minutes until darkened.
Add remaining stock, simmer 15 minutes, and season.
Meanwhile, fry the tortilla strips in the remaining oil until crisp; drain.
Add chicken to the soup to warm through and ladle into bowls topped with tortilla strips, avocado, queso fresco, crema, and lime.

recipes.title: Sopes
recipes.description: Thick masa cakes with pinched edges, topped with refried beans, meat, lettuce, salsa, crema, and cheese.
steps: Knead masa harina, warm water, and salt to a smooth dough; rest 10 minutes covered.
Divide into 12 balls and press each between plastic into 8 cm rounds about 6 mm thick.
Cook on a dry comal over medium heat for 1 minute per side until set but not browned.
While still warm, pinch the edges up to form a small rim like a low boat.
Brush or shallow-fry sopes briefly in oil until lightly golden and slightly crisp.
Spread each with refried beans and top with shredded meat.
Finish with lettuce, salsa, crumbled queso fresco, and a drizzle of crema.

recipes.title: Cochinita Pibil
recipes.description: Yucatecan slow-roasted pork marinated in achiote and sour orange, wrapped in banana leaves until meltingly tender.

ingredients.name: sour orange juice (or 50/50 orange and lime juice)

steps: Blend achiote paste, sour orange juice, garlic, cumin, oregano, and salt to a thick marinade.
Toss the pork with the marinade and refrigerate at least 4 hours, ideally overnight.
Pass the banana leaves over an open flame briefly to make them pliable, then line a Dutch oven, leaving long overhang.
Add the pork and marinade, fold the leaves over to enclose, and cover tightly.
Bake at 150°C for 4 hours until the pork shreds easily.
While it cooks, mix sliced red onion, habanero, lime juice, and salt for a quick pickle and rest 30 minutes.
Shred the pork and serve with warm tortillas and pickled onions.

recipes.title: Tinga de Pollo
recipes.description: Smoky shredded chicken simmered with tomato and chipotle, served on tostadas or in tacos.
steps: Simmer chicken in salted water with garlic and a piece of onion for 18 minutes; cool and shred.
Char tomatoes on a comal until blistered, then blend with chipotles and 2 cloves garlic until smooth.
Heat oil in a pan and sauté the sliced onion until soft.
Pour in the tomato-chipotle puree and oregano and fry 5 minutes.
Add the shredded chicken and stock and simmer 12 minutes until the sauce coats the meat thickly.
Spread tostadas with refried beans, top with tinga, lettuce, queso fresco, and crema.
Serve immediately while crispy.

recipes.title: Birria
recipes.description: Jalisco-style braised meat in a deep red chile broth, served as stew or in cheese-stuffed quesabirria tacos.
steps: Toast and soak all chiles in hot water 15 minutes.
Char tomatoes, half the onion, and garlic on a comal until soft.
Blend chiles, charred vegetables, vinegar, cumin, oregano, cinnamon, and cloves with a little soaking water until very smooth; strain.
Toss the beef with the chile sauce in a Dutch oven, add bay leaves and stock, and bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook in a 160°C oven for 3 hours until the meat shreds easily.
Shred meat and skim the bright red consommé.
For quesabirria, dip tortillas in the consommé fat, place on a hot griddle, fill with meat and cheese, fold, and crisp on both sides; serve tacos with a small bowl of consommé for dipping.

recipes.title: Chicharrón en Salsa Verde
recipes.description: Crispy pork rinds simmered briefly in tangy green tomatillo salsa until tender; served with tortillas.
steps: Boil tomatillos, chiles, onion, and garlic until soft, about 8 minutes.
Drain and blend with cilantro and salt to a smooth salsa.
Heat oil in a deep skillet, pour in the salsa, and fry for 5 minutes until darker green.
Break the chicharrón into bite-sized pieces and add to the salsa.
Simmer gently 8–10 minutes until the chicharrón is just softened but not mushy.
Adjust salt.
Serve immediately with hot corn tortillas.

recipes.title: Quesadilla
recipes.description: Folded corn or flour tortilla filled with melted cheese and optional fillings like squash blossom or huitlacoche.
steps: Heat a comal or non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Warm one tortilla, sprinkle one half generously with cheese, and add a leaf of epazote or other filling.
Fold the tortilla over and press lightly with a spatula.
Cook 1–2 minutes per side until the tortilla is lightly toasted and the cheese is fully melted.
Repeat with remaining tortillas, brushing the pan with a little oil only if needed.
Cut in half and serve hot with salsa verde.

recipes.title: Elote
recipes.description: Mexican grilled corn on the cob slathered with mayo, crema, cotija cheese, lime, and chile powder.
steps: Heat a grill to high or use a stovetop grill pan.
Grill the corn, turning often, until charred in spots all around, about 8–10 minutes.
Mix mayo and crema in a small bowl.
Brush the hot corn generously with the mayo mixture, coating all sides.
Roll each cob in cotija to coat thickly.
Sprinkle with chile powder and cilantro and squeeze lime over the top.
Serve immediately on sticks or as is.

recipes.title: Esquites
recipes.description: Mexican corn salad with the same flavors as elote, served warm in a cup with mayo, lime, cotija, and chile.
steps: Melt butter in a wide skillet over high heat.
Add the corn and onion in a single layer and cook undisturbed for 4 minutes to char.
Stir and cook another 3 minutes until kernels are tender and browned in spots.
Stir in epazote and remove from heat.
In a bowl, fold the corn with mayo, lime juice, half the cotija, salt, and chile powder.
Spoon into cups, top with remaining cotija, more chile powder, and cilantro.
Serve warm.

recipes.title: Sopa de Fideo
recipes.description: Mexican comfort soup of toasted thin noodles in a tomato-chicken broth, often topped with avocado and queso fresco.
steps: Blend tomatoes, onion, and garlic with a little water until smooth.
Heat oil in a pot and toast the fideo, stirring constantly until evenly golden brown, about 4 minutes.
Pour in the tomato puree and fry for 3 minutes until slightly darkened.
Add chicken stock and salt and bring to a simmer.
Cook 8 minutes until the noodles are tender and the soup has thickened slightly.
Adjust salt and ladle into bowls.
Top with avocado, queso fresco, and a squeeze of lime.

recipes.title: Arroz Rojo
recipes.description: Classic Mexican red rice gently fried then simmered in tomato broth with carrots and peas.
steps: Rinse the rice well in cool water and drain thoroughly.
Blend tomatoes, onion, and garlic with a splash of water until smooth.
Heat oil in a pot and fry the rice, stirring, until milky white and lightly toasted, about 6 minutes.
Pour in the tomato puree and fry 2 minutes.
Add stock, carrot, peas, and salt; bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce to low, and cook 18 minutes.
Rest covered 10 minutes, then fluff with a fork.

recipes.title: Frijoles Charros
recipes.description: Hearty 'cowboy beans' simmered with bacon, sausage, tomatoes, and chiles, served as a brothy side or light meal.
steps: Drain the soaked beans, cover with fresh water, and simmer 1.5 hours until tender; reserve cooking liquid.
In another pot, render the bacon until crisp.
Add chorizo and brown, breaking it up.
Add onion and cook 4 minutes until soft, then garlic and jalapeños for 1 minute.
Stir in tomatoes and cook 5 minutes until they break down.
Add the beans with enough cooking liquid to make a brothy stew and simmer 15 minutes.
Stir in cilantro, season, and serve in bowls.

recipes.title: Flan
recipes.description: Silky baked Mexican custard with a layer of golden caramel sauce on top when unmolded.
steps: Heat sugar and water in a saucepan over medium heat without stirring until the sugar dissolves and turns a deep amber, about 8 minutes.
Quickly pour the caramel into a 23 cm round baking dish and tilt to coat the bottom; let cool to set.
Preheat the oven to 160°C.
Blend whole milk, condensed milk, evaporated milk, eggs, vanilla, and salt until smooth.
Strain through a fine sieve into the caramel-lined dish.
Place the dish in a roasting pan, pour hot water halfway up the sides, and bake 60–70 minutes until the center is set with a slight wobble.
Cool, then refrigerate at least 6 hours; run a knife around the edge and invert onto a plate so the caramel pools over the flan.

recipes.title: Churros
recipes.description: Fried dough sticks tossed in cinnamon sugar, often served with thick Mexican hot chocolate for dipping.
steps: Bring water, butter, sugar, and salt to a boil.
Add all the flour at once and stir vigorously until the dough pulls away from the sides; cool 5 minutes.
Beat in the egg and vanilla until smooth and shiny.
Transfer dough to a piping bag fitted with a large star tip.
Heat oil to 175°C and pipe 12 cm strips directly into the oil, snipping with kitchen shears.
Fry until deep golden, about 4 minutes, turning once.
Drain briefly and toss in cinnamon sugar while warm; serve immediately.

recipes.title: Horchata
recipes.description: Refreshing Mexican rice and almond drink flavored with cinnamon and vanilla, served over ice.
steps: Combine rice, almonds, and cinnamon stick in a bowl, cover with the warm water, and soak at least 4 hours or overnight.
Pour the entire mixture into a blender and blend on high for 2 minutes until very smooth.
Strain through a fine sieve or cheesecloth into a pitcher, pressing to extract liquid; discard solids.
Whisk in condensed milk, whole milk, and vanilla.
Taste and adjust sweetness.
Refrigerate at least 1 hour.
Pour over ice and dust with ground cinnamon to serve.
